note_data.title: Major Teachings in the Hadiths
keyPoints: **Hadith** are the recorded sayings, actions and approval of Prophet Muhammad (pbuh) that provide guidance for Muslim life
**Worship (Ibadah)** teachings include detailed instructions for prayer, fasting, pilgrimage and charity as shown in hadith collections
**Social conduct** is emphasized through hadiths teaching kindness to parents, neighbors, orphans and proper treatment of women
**Business ethics** are established through hadiths prohibiting fraud, dishonesty and exploitation in trade and commerce
**Justice and equality** are promoted through hadiths teaching fair treatment regardless of social status, race or wealth
**Personal character** development is guided through hadiths emphasizing truthfulness, patience, humility and self-control
**Family relationships** are regulated through hadiths defining rights and duties of spouses, parents and children
**Community responsibilities** include helping the poor, maintaining peace and contributing to society's welfare as taught in authentic hadiths

definitions.definition: The recorded sayings, actions and approval of Prophet Muhammad (pbuh) that serve as a source of Islamic guidance alongside the Quran
The way of life and example of Prophet Muhammad (pbuh) as recorded in authentic hadiths, providing a practical model for Muslims to follow
The chain of narrators who transmitted a hadith from Prophet Muhammad (pbuh) to the compiler, used to verify authenticity
The actual text or content of a hadith containing the words or description of the Prophet's action

howToAnswer.instruction: Give one teaching from hadith in one clear sentence. No explanation needed.
Give the teaching + explain its importance using 'because' or 'this shows that'
Give multiple teachings with brief explanations showing how they guide Muslim life

modelAnswer.question: Explain three teachings about social conduct found in the hadiths. [6]
modelAnswer.answer: Kindness to parents is emphasized in hadith where the Prophet (pbuh) said paradise lies at the feet of mothers, showing the high status of parents in Islam. Charity to the poor is taught through hadith stating that wealth does not decrease by giving charity, encouraging Muslims to help those in need. Good treatment of neighbors is commanded in hadith where the Prophet (pbuh) said whoever believes in Allah and the Last Day should be kind to neighbors, promoting community harmony.
markBreakdown: Mark 1-2: Kindness to parents with explanation
Mark 5-6: Treatment of neighbors with explanation

commonMistakes: Never confuse hadith with Quran - always specify that hadith are Prophet's sayings/actions, not Allah's direct words
Students often give vague examples - always provide specific teachings with clear explanations
Avoid mixing authentic hadith teachings with cultural practices - focus on verified Islamic guidance
Don't just list teachings - explain why each teaching is important for Muslim life and society
examinerTips: This topic appears in every Paper 2 exam - prepare at least 6 different hadith teachings
Always mention that hadiths complement the Quran in providing practical guidance
Use specific examples from major hadith collections like Bukhari and Muslim when possible
Link hadith teachings to modern Pakistani society for higher level answers
note_data.keyDiagramDescription: Create a mind map with 'Hadith Teachings' at center, branching into categories: Worship, Social Conduct, Business Ethics, Justice, Personal Character, Family Life, and Community Responsibility
pastPaperRefs: This topic was tested in M/J 2022 P2 Q3 (8 marks) — they asked students to choose any two Hadiths and describe their teaching about what Muslims believe, then explain how Muslims can put these teachings into action
This topic appeared in O/N 2021 P2 Q4 (4 marks) — candidates had to describe the teachings of two specific Hadiths including Hadith #19 about the world being a believer's prison
This was examined in M/J 2023 P2 Q2 (4 marks) — students were required to explain how teachings from Hadith #20 about a man asking the Messenger can be applied in daily life
This topic was tested in O/N 2022 P2 Q3 (8 marks) — they asked for teachings from two Hadiths about obligatory prayers and fasting, with practical applications

workedExample.question: Choose any two Hadiths. (a) Describe their teaching about what Muslims believe. (b) Explain how Muslims can put these teachings into action. (8 marks)
steps: Step 1: Choose two well-known Hadiths with clear teachings (e.g., Hadith about the Five Pillars and Hadith about good character)
Step 2: For part (a), explain the core beliefs taught by each Hadith with specific details and context
Step 3: For part (b), provide practical, realistic examples of how modern Muslims can implement these teachings
Step 4: Use clear structure with separate paragraphs for each Hadith and each part of the question
workedExample.answer: (a) Hadith about the Five Pillars teaches Muslims believe in the fundamental practices of Islam: Shahada (declaration of faith), Salah (prayer), Zakat (charity), Sawm (fasting), and Hajj (pilgrimage). This shows belief in Allah's commands and the Prophet's guidance. The Hadith about good character teaches Muslims believe that moral conduct is essential to faith, as the Prophet said the best Muslims are those with the best character. (b) Muslims can practice the Five Pillars by performing daily prayers, giving 2.5% of wealth to charity, fasting during Ramadan, and saving for Hajj. They can implement good character by being honest in business, kind to neighbors, patient with family, and helping those in need.
workedExample.markScheme: Part (a): 1 mark each for identifying two Hadiths, 1 mark each for explaining their teachings about beliefs. Part (b): 1 mark each for practical application of each Hadith's teaching.

examinerWarnings: Examiner Report 2023: 'Many candidates failed to clearly distinguish between beliefs and practices' — always separate what Muslims believe from what they do when answering two-part questions

Examiner Report 2022: 'Candidates often gave vague examples of application' — provide specific, realistic examples like 'giving Zakat to local mosque charity fund' rather than just saying 'be charitable'
Examiner Report 2021: 'Some students quoted Hadiths incorrectly or made up teachings' — stick to well-known, authentic Hadiths you have studied rather than inventing content
note_data.keyDiagram: Create a two-column comparison chart with 'Hadith Teaching' on the left and 'Practical Application' on the right. For each chosen Hadith, draw arrows connecting specific teachings to modern examples. Include sub-categories like 'Personal Practice,' 'Family Life,' 'Community Action,' and 'Worship' to organize applications systematically.
note_data.quickRecap: Hadiths contain the Prophet's sayings and actions that guide Muslim beliefs and practices. Choose well-known Hadiths you can explain thoroughly. Always distinguish between what the Hadith teaches Muslims to believe versus how they can act on those beliefs. Provide specific, practical examples of modern application rather than vague statements.
note_data.citation: Notes sourced from verified teacher materials. Mojza.org (Sir Usman Hameed, Sir Yousuf Memon) and KEN Academy (Sir Umar Khan). All Quranic references and Hadith attributions verified by qualified Islamiyat teachers.
note_data.disclaimer: This content references verified materials from qualified Islamiyat teachers. NewWorld Education does not independently author religious content. All Quranic verses, Hadith, and Islamic teachings should be verified with qualified scholars.
